title: Population | Stats NZ
content:
Population statistics give you information about people who live in New Zealand.
Find out the number of people in New Zealand and in different areas of New Zealand. These areas include regions, cities and towns, local board areas, and area units/Statistical Area 2.
Information is broken down by groups within the population, for example by age, ethnicity, and sex.
Population statistics also include information on births and deaths, migration, and census results.
For advice on using the different population statistics, see Population statistics – user guide .
Population estimates and projections show how the population is changing. The latest population estimates are based on the 2023 Census, following the release of the Estimated resident population (2023-base): At 30 June 2023 on 16 April 2025.
Estimated resident population of New Zealand at 31 March 2026
Estimated resident population

## Latest information releases about population

## LGBT+ population of Aotearoa New Zealand: Year ended June 2025
LGBT+ population of Aotearoa New Zealand provides key demographic and economic characteristics of the LGBT+ population as a whole, of the transgender and non-binary populations, and of the sexual minorities’ populations. The statistics include qualifications and income, as well as disability status for those who are part of the LGBT+ population.

## LGBT+ population of Aotearoa New Zealand: Year ended June 2024
LGBT+ population of Aotearoa New Zealand provides key demographic and economic characteristics of the LGBT+ population as a whole, of the transgender and non-binary population, and of the sexual minorities’ population. The statistics include qualifications and income, as well as disability status for those who are part of the LGBT+ population.

## Māori population estimates: Mean year ended 31 December 2025
Māori population estimates give the best available measure of the Māori ethnic population, by age and sex, usually living in Aotearoa New Zealand.

## Births and deaths: Year ended March 2026
Births and deaths releases provide statistics on the number of births and deaths of people resident in New Zealand that are registered during a given period, along with selected fertility and mortality rates. They may differ from statistics presented elsewhere that relate to all births and deaths registered in New Zealand or to births and deaths occurring during a given period.

## Latest publications about population

## LGBT+ population younger, income varies between groups
The latest data from the Household Income and Living Survey estimated that 4.8 percent of those aged 15 years and over identified as LGBT+ in the year ended June 2025.

## Applying the Stats NZ data updates policy to outputs from the Household Economic Survey
Looks at how the Stats NZ data updates policy is applied to the Household Economic Survey (HES).

## Applying the Stats NZ data updates policy to outputs from the Household Income and Living Survey
Read about how the Stats NZ data updates policy is applied to the Household Income and Living Survey (HILS).

## Net migration gain of 24,200
New Zealand had a net migration gain of 24,200 in the March 2026 year, up from a net gain of 14,000 in the March 2025 year.

### Download historical population summary figures
Data from 1991 onwards (year ended 31 December) is available in the following Excel file.

## Tools with data about population
The population clock estimates New Zealand's population in real time.

## How long will I live?
Get an indication of the age you’re likely to live to.

## Aotearoa Data Explorer
A data tool to search for data, create tables customising the variables and layout, and download Excel or CSV files.

## Place and ethnic group summaries
Explore statistics about the population by region, district, and smaller areas, and by ethnic group.

## Interactive population pyramid for New Zealand
Use this interactive population pyramid to see our population history and what our future may look like.

## 2023 Census product and release finder
The 2023 Census product and release finder is an Excel-based tool which provides easy-to-find data, statistical products, publications, and planned release dates in one place for the 2023 Census.

## 2013 Census QuickStats about a place
Find out how to get 2013 Census statistics by place.
Build tables from our biggest range of datasets, including long-term time series. View on screen or download Excel or CSV files.

## Most common birthday in New Zealand
Find out how many New Zealanders you share your birthday with.

## 2013 Census iwi profiles
Find statistics from the 2013 Census about iwi and iwi groupings.

## 2013 Census ethnic group profiles
Find statistics from the 2013 Census about the different ethnic groups living in New Zealand.
Find and download concepts and definitions, classifications, concordances, and standards used for data and statistical activities across government.

## Geographic data service
Search, browse, and download geographic boundaries and maps for New Zealand, plus a selection of 2018 and 2013 Census spatial data.
Get statistical metadata – what we measure, definitions, and how we produce statistics.
Explore and visualise statistics about New Zealand and its people in map form.

## NZ progress indicators
The data in these indicators is no longer being updated. Get a picture of how well we are living, how resources are being distributed and used, and what we are leaving for future generations.

## NZ social indicators
The data in these indicators is no longer being updated. Explore data on a range of key New Zealand social statistics.
Estimated resident population (ERP) at 30 June 2018
Find out about the distribution of people at 30 June 2018, plus proportions in different broad age and ethnic groups, for regions, territorial authority and Auckland local board areas, statistical area 2 (SA2s), and urban rural. The data behind these maps can be accessed from Datafinder .
Population grids for New Zealand
Trial a new geospatial format for delivering population data – the population grid.

## Information about data
Get technical information such as classifications used, survey design, and a glossary of statistical terms.

### Metadata in DataInfo+
DataInfo+ has metadata about our statistical collections and classifications, and questionnaires and forms.
DataInfo+ concepts gives definitions of terms used by Stats NZ.
Births – DataInfo+ gives the general methodology used to produce birth statistics, and definitions and related metadata.
Deaths – DataInfo+ explains the general methodology used to produce death statistics, and definitions and related metadata.
Demographic estimates – DataInfo+
Demographic projections – DataInfo+
International travel and migration – DataInfo+ explains the general methodology used to produce ITM statistics, and definitions and related metadata.
New Zealand life tables – DataInfo+ explains the general methodology used to produce life table statistics, and definitions and related metadata.

### Methods, sources, and user guides
Population statistics – user guide provides information about population statistics we produce.
Standard for population terms will help you understand and use Stats NZ's population measures.
Evaluation of the accuracy of Stats NZ population estimates and projections, 1996–2018 is designed to help customers understand the accuracy of Stats NZ’s population estimates and projections relative to observed populations, the reasons for inaccuracies, and discusses current developments that may improve future accuracy.
- Gender sex and intersex
- Population estimates and projections
